title: geum calthifolium var. dilatatum
intro: Geum calthifolium var. dilatatum, commonly known as Korean avens, is a variety of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, due to its health benefits. The therapeutic actions of Geum calthifolium var. dilatatum include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its bioactive constituents, such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. side_effects: Geum calthifolium var. dilatatum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of anthraquinone glycosides. In excessive doses, it may also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin irritation, itching, and hives, in some individuals. Additionally, using Geum calthifolium var. dilatatum improperly can interact with certain medications, like blood thinners, and decrease their effectiveness or increase the risk of bleeding. In severe cases, improper use can also lead to more serious complications, like kidney damage, if not monitored and managed properly.
